chief_complaint: A 73-year-old man presented with exertional chest pain.
history: The patient had a history of severe stenosis of the right coronary artery (RCA) and severe stenotic lesions in the proximal and distal RCA. He reported a metal allergy and had moderate allergic reactions to Ni- and CoCr-EES confirmed by patch tests. He refused coronary artery bypass surgery and underwent percutaneous coronary intervention (PCI) with a CoCr-SES with PROBIO coating.
exam: Vital signs and physical examination findings are not provided.
labs: Coronary computed tomography revealed severe stenosis of the RCA, and coronary angiography (CAG) revealed severe stenotic lesions in the proximal and distal RCA. Intravascular ultrasound showed positive remodeling, a plaque with echo attenuation, and a mural thrombus at the culprit lesion. Follow-up CAG 56 days after PCI showed good stent patency, and optical coherence tomography imaging showed coverage of 92% of the struts with no obvious thrombus formation. Follow-up CAG 408 days after PCI showed coverage of 98% of the struts with no peri-stent contrast staining.
